sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see more clearly what's happening:
1. Purple diamond moved from [36, 42] to [33, 42] (moved up)
2. White diamond disappeared from [40, 40] (W(10) is gone)
3. Gray diamond at [33, 45] changed from G(10) to G(4) - it shrank further from 10 to 4 pixels!
4. The W(53) at [11, 0] is the HUD element (changed from W(54) at [10, 0])
So clicking on the purple diamond:
- Moves the purple diamond up
- Shrinks the gray diamond at [33, 45] (from 12 -> 10 -> 4)
- The white diamond at [40, 40] disappeared
Wait, the white diamond at [40, 40] (W(10)) is gone now. Did it merge with something or disappear? Let me check if there's a white diamond elsewhere.
Looking at the current objects, I don't see a W(12) or W(10) object. The only white object is W(53) which is the HUD element.
So the white diamond disappeared. This is strange.
Actually, wait. Let me look at the gray diamond at [34, 46] which is now G(4). Previously it was G(10) at [33, 45]. So the gray diamond shrank and moved slightly.
Maybe the white diamond merged into the gray diamond? Or maybe the white diamond was "absorbed" by the purple diamond?
Let me think about this differently. The pattern seems to be:
1. Click on gray diamond -> white diamond swaps with it
2. Click on orange diamond outline -> white diamond moves inside it, purple diamond moves
3. Click on purple diamond -> purple diamond moves up, gray diamond shrinks, white diamond disappears
Maybe the goal is to shrink all gray diamonds to 0? Or to eliminate all gray diamonds?
Currently:
- G(12) at [4, 17]
- G(12) at [7, 49]
- G(12) at [19, 8]
- G(4) at [34, 46] (was G(12), shrunk twice)
So one gray diamond has been shrunk from 12 to 4. If I continue clicking on the purple diamond, it might shrink further to 0.
But wait, the purple diamond is at [33, 42] and the gray diamond is at [34, 46]. They're close but not adjacent.
Let me try clicking on the purple diamond again to see if the gray diamond continues to shrink.
